stem: A software testing study surveys 25 test cases and observes 7 positive outcomes out of 25 (28.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (13 observations, rate = 15.4%) and Group B (12 observations, rate = 41.7%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 36% and 47% of the population. Estimate the true software bug rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.28. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.3131 (range [0.2979, 0.3257]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.1428, 0.4758]. Point estimate: 0.3131.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.41: 0.41×0.164 + 0.59×0.4167 = 0.3131.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=25, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
